In Deuteronomy 32:49, which mountain does the Lord tell Moses to climb to view Canaan?

32.title: Moses sees but does not enter
32.text: Because he trespassed at the waters of Meribah, Moses views the promised land from Mount Nebo but dies without entering it.

33.title: From Sinai to Paran
33.text: The poem opens by picturing God coming from Sinai, from Seir, and from mount Paran — an image of divine majesty sweeping over the desert landmarks of Israel's wandering.

33.question: Levi's blessing links that tribe to which sacred objects used to discern God's will?

33.question: Speaking of Joseph, Moses names "the ten thousands of Ephraim" and "the thousands of Manasseh." Who were Ephraim and Manasseh?

33.title: The everlasting arms
33.text: The image of "the eternal God" as a refuge and of "the everlasting arms" underneath Israel became one of the most quoted phrases of the book, evoking divine protection and support.

34.question: To whom did the Lord say He had sworn to give this land for their seed?

34.title: An unknown grave
34.text: The account says the Lord Himself buried Moses in a valley in Moab, and that to the day it was written no man knew the exact place of his sepulchre.
